questions.question: What is the topic for Week 38?
questions.explanation: The topic explicitly listed for Lesson Thirty-Eight is CHRISTIANITY AND HOMOSEXUALITY (Part 2).

questions.question: Which of the following was the topic for Week 37?
questions.explanation: Christianity and Homosexuality (Part 1) was the topic for Week 37.

questions.question: If a man lies with a male as he lies with a woman, both of them have committed an abomination. They shall surely be put to ________.
questions.explanation: The memory verse (Leviticus 20:13) says 'They shall surely be put to death.'

questions.question: Provide the book, chapter, and verse for this week's memory verse.
questions.explanation: The memory verse reference is Leviticus 20:13.

questions.question: Type out the memory verse for Week 38 in full (NKJV).
questions.answer: If a man lies with a male as he lies with a woman, both of them have committed an abomination. They shall surely be put to death. Their blood shall be upon them.
questions.explanation: Full text recall of Leviticus 20:13.

questions.question: According to the memory verse (Leviticus 20:13), what shall be upon those who commit this abomination?
questions.explanation: The verse ends with: 'Their blood shall be upon them.'

questions.question: What two things does the introduction say the Week 38 lesson will examine?
options: A. The history of homosexuality and modern laws
B. The dangers involved and believers' disposition to homosexuals
C. How to protest and write letters to politicians
D. The origin of the Greek word and its translation
questions.explanation: The introduction states 'we shall examine the dangers involved and believers’ disposition to homosexuals.'

questions.question: What is the heading of the first outline?
questions.explanation: Outline I is titled 'THE DANGERS INVOLVED'.

questions.question: According to Outline I, what will this sexual pervasion result in regarding God and the perpetrator's heart?
options: A. Abandonment from God and the hardening of the perpetrator's heart
C. God giving them great wealth to test them
D. God softening their heart immediately
questions.explanation: The text states 'The Scripture mentions that this sexual pervasion will result in abandonment from God and the hardening of the perpetrator’s heart (Romans 1:28).'

questions.question: God will give them over to continue in things that are 'not convenient', which means inappropriate and shameful.
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states 'God will give them over to continue in things that are ‘not convenient’ i.e. ‘not becoming’, ‘inappropriate’, ‘shameful’.'

questions.question: According to the logical progression in Outline I, without an awareness of their sinfulness, there will be no what?
questions.explanation: The text states 'Without an awareness of their sinfulness, there will be no repentance (2 Corinthians 4:3-4).'

questions.question: Continuing the progression from Outline I, without forgiveness, there is no what?
questions.explanation: The text concludes the sequence with: 'Without forgiveness, there is no salvation (2 Corinthians 7:10; Romans 10:10).'

questions.question: What is the heading of the second outline?
questions.explanation: Outline II is titled 'CHRISTIANS' DISPOSITION'.

questions.question: According to Outline II, what are Christians called to demonstrate towards all, including homosexuals?
questions.explanation: The text states 'Christians are called to demonstrate the love of Christ towards all, including homosexuals (Matthew 22:39).'

questions.question: Treating homosexuals with dignity and praying for their salvation means that Christians should approve of their sin.
questions.explanation: The text explicitly says 'However, this does not mean that Christians should approve of their sin (Ephesians 5:11; 1 Timothy 5:22b).'

questions.question: Why should Christians treat homosexuals with the same dignity as they would anyone else?
options: B. Because it is illegal not to
C. Because they are also made in the image of God
D. Because they might be wealthy
questions.explanation: The text states 'They should treat homosexuals with the same dignity as they would anyone else because they are also made in the image of God...'

questions.question: According to the Week 38 lesson, what does our society rely on for its moral truth?
questions.explanation: The text says 'Although, our society does not rely on the Bible for its moral truth but on humanistic and relativistic morals...'

questions.question: According to Outline II, it takes a huge 'leap of logic' to discern that homosexual marriage is also condemned in the Bible.
questions.explanation: The text states 'It takes no ‘leap of logic’ to discern that homosexual marriage is also condemned.'

questions.question: According to the conclusion, what should flow through you so that sinners can see it and turn to Christ?
questions.explanation: The conclusion says 'Let the love of Christ flow through you so that sinners can see true love and turn to Christ.'

questions.question: Where is this verse found? 'Say to them: ‘As I live,’ says the Lord God, ‘I have no pleasure in the death of the wicked, but that the wicked turn from his way and live...’'
questions.verse_text: Say to them: ‘As I live,’ says the Lord God, ‘I have no pleasure in the death of the wicked, but that the wicked turn from his way and live. Turn, turn from your evil ways! For why should you die, O house of Israel?’
questions.explanation: This is verse 11 from the main Bible Passage.

questions.question: According to the Bible Passage (Ezekiel 33:11), what does the Lord God have no pleasure in?
options: B. The death of the wicked
C. The prosperity of the proud
D. The prayers of the unrighteous
questions.explanation: Verse 11 says 'I have no pleasure in the death of the wicked...'

questions.question: According to Ezekiel 33:12, will the righteousness of the righteous man deliver him in the day of his transgression?
options: B. Only if his good deeds outweigh his bad deeds
C. No, it shall not deliver him
D. Yes, if he offers a sacrifice
questions.explanation: Verse 12 says 'The righteousness of the righteous man shall not deliver him in the day of his transgression...'

questions.question: According to Ezekiel 33:13, if the righteous man trusts in his own righteousness and commits iniquity, what will happen to his righteous works?
options: A. They will be heavily rewarded
B. They will cancel out his iniquity
C. None of his righteous works shall be remembered
D. They will be remembered for a generation
questions.explanation: Verse 13 says 'none of his righteous works shall be remembered; but because of the iniquity that he has committed, he shall die.'

questions.question: According to Ezekiel 33:14-15, if the wicked turns from his sin, does what is lawful and right, restores the pledge, and gives back what he stole, what is the result?
options: A. He shall die a painful death
B. He shall surely live; he shall not die
C. He must leave the country
D. He must pay double to the priests
questions.explanation: Verse 15 concludes 'he shall surely live; he shall not die.'

questions.question: Which scriptures are cited in Outline I to show that they can no longer see the effects of their wrongdoings?
questions.explanation: Outline I cites '(Proverbs 10:17; Proverbs 14:12)' regarding not seeing the effects of their wrongdoings.

questions.question: Therefore, every Christian and the church at large must continue to speak the truth in ________ and pray for sinners until they come to the knowledge of Christ.
questions.explanation: Outline II says 'must continue to speak the truth in love...'

questions.question: Homosexuality is a sin, and like any other sin, it needs to be laid at the cross and repented of.
questions.explanation: Outline II explicitly states 'Homosexuality is a sin and like any other sin, it needs to be laid at the cross and repented of (Romans 6:6; Ephesians 4:22-24).'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited to support that without repentance, there will be no forgiveness?
questions.explanation: Outline I cites '(Acts 8:22)' next to 'Without repentance, there will be no forgiveness'.

questions.question: Which scriptures are cited to remind Christians not to approve of their sin?
questions.explanation: Outline II cites '(Ephesians 5:11; 1 Timothy 5:22b)' right after 'does not mean that Christians should approve of their sin'.

questions.question: According to the Week 38 lesson, how should Christians pray for the salvation of homosexuals?
options: B. The same way they would any other person in sin
C. By demanding immediate fire from heaven
D. With special fasting rituals only
questions.explanation: Outline II states 'Christians should pray for the salvation of homosexuals the same way they would any other person in sin...'
